page: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core.

For acceleration, the instance includes a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, featuring 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 250 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate highly specialized performance characteristics. The instance achieves top-tier status in large language model inference speeds, processing 13365.51 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model and 6130.77 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Conversely, general compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks show some limitations. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, but multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with Geekbench multi-core and software compilation tests, register in the weak category.

The hardware profile represents a qualitative tradeoff, offering high-density GPU acceleration and VRAM alongside a moderate CPU and memory bandwidth allocation. This makes the instance cost-efficient for workloads that heavily offload computation to the GPU rather than relying on high-throughput system memory or massive multi-core CPU processing. The instance is suited for small to medium LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, and GPU-accelerated workloads, while being less optimal for memory-bandwidth-bound database operations or heavy multi-threaded CPU compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is an x86_64 graphics-intensive instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM and a 250 GB local NVMe SSD. While general CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average and weak categories, the instance delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference, processing over 6,130 tokens per second for prompt processing on 7B models. This hardware profile presents a qualitative tradeoff, offering high GPU capabilities alongside moderate CPU resources. It is suited for GPU-accelerated tasks, small to medium LLM inference, and graphics-intensive workloads, but is less optimal for memory-bandwidth-sensitive database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, a 250 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: An accelerated instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing with an NVIDIA L40S GPU and local NVMe storage for specialized workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB DDR4 RAM
NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM
250 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference
Suited for graphics-intensive applications and GPU-accelerated tasks
